seoTitle: How to Build a Wix Website From a Blank Template
metaDescription: Learn how to start a Wix website from a completely blank canvas, adding sections, text, images, and navigation from scratch.
intro: Starting from a blank Wix template gives you full creative control instead of adapting a pre-made design. This guide covers finding the blank canvas option, structuring your page with layout strips, and adding the core elements like navigation, headers, and images so you can build a fully custom site from the ground up.

steps.heading: Open the templates dashboard
steps.body: From your Wix workspace, open the All Templates category to browse your starting options.

steps.heading: Choose Start from Scratch
steps.body: Select the blank templates submenu and click Edit on the empty canvas layout.

steps.heading: Review the raw canvas structure
steps.body: Identify your header, body, and footer grid bounds before adding any content.

steps.heading: Add your first layout strip
steps.body: Add a background strip layer to begin dividing your page into organized sections.

steps.heading: Configure navigation and header elements
steps.body: Set up your main navigation bar, logo, and typography styles.

steps.heading: Add images and buttons, then preview
steps.body: Deploy image containers and interactive buttons, check the mobile view for responsiveness, and preview before publishing.

faq.question: Is building from a blank template harder than using a pre-made one?
faq.answer: It takes more manual layout work since you're placing every element yourself, but it gives you complete control over the design.

faq.question: Can I still use Wix's design tools on a blank canvas?
faq.answer: Yes, all the standard sidebar tools for text, images, and sections are available — you're just starting with an empty page instead of pre-filled content.

faq.question: How do I make sure my blank layout looks good on mobile?
faq.answer: Toggle the mobile view mode regularly while building to check that your sections and text scale properly across screen sizes.

faq.question: Can I add a navigation menu to a blank template?
faq.answer: Yes, you'll need to manually build and configure your navigation bar since a blank template doesn't include one by default.
